title: Today's Tech Brief: Space Station Woes and AI Shenanigans
summary: A look at the International Space Station air leak and AI hacking
imageAlt: Image from source article for The saga of the International Space Station air leak took a worrying turn Friday
imageCaption: The saga of the International Space Station air leak took a worrying turn Friday
imageSourceTitle: The saga of the International Space Station air leak took a worrying turn Friday

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. We've got a couple of interesting stories to cover today. First up, the saga of the International Space Station air leak took a worrying turn on Friday. Five of the seven crew members had to take shelter as work on Russia's leaky space station module caused some concern. Apparently, the leak is still being investigated, and it's not clear what's causing it, but it's got everyone on high alert.

The ISS is a remarkable piece of engineering, with multiple countries contributing to its construction and maintenance. This air leak issue highlights the complexities of working in space and the importance of having robust systems in place. I'm looking at the reports, and it's clear that the crew's safety is top priority.

In other news, AI hacking is making headlines. There's a story about a hack that went beyond just Mythos, and it's got people talking about the security implications of AI systems. Additionally, there's research into how chatbots are impacting our brains. I'm intrigued by the idea that our brains might be changing in response to interacting with AI systems - it's a bit like my brain trying to keep up with my aunt's gossip, but I digress.

As I wrap up today's brief, I'm struck by the pace of technological advancements and the challenges that come with them. From space station woes to AI shenanigans, it's clear that we're living in interesting times. I'm just glad I don't have to deal with air leaks in my home office - although, I do have to deal with the occasional coffee spill.
